mcqs.question: Which of the following statements best describes Newton's First Law of Motion?
options: A. An object in motion stays in motion unless acted upon by an external force.
B. Force equals mass times acceleration.
C. For every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction.
D. An object at rest will remain at rest unless acted upon by an external force.

mcqs.question: What is inertia?
options: A. The tendency of an object to resist changes in its state of motion.
B. The force that causes an object to move.
C. The weight of an object.
D. The speed of an object.

mcqs.question: What happens to a stationary object when no external force acts on it?
options: A. It starts moving.
B. It remains stationary.
C. It accelerates.
D. It changes direction.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is an example of Newton's First Law of Motion?
options: A. A car accelerating when the driver presses the gas pedal.
B. A book sliding off a table.
C. A ball rolling on the ground eventually stopping due to friction.
D. A passenger continuing to move forward when the bus suddenly stops.

mcqs.question: In which situation does an object experience inertia?
options: A. When it is in motion.
B. When it is at rest.
C. Both A and B.
D. When it is falling.

fill_in_the_blanks.question: An object at rest will remain at rest unless acted upon by an __________ force.
The property of an object to resist changes in its state of motion is called __________.
According to Newton's First Law, an object in motion will continue in its state of motion unless acted upon by an __________ force.
The First Law of Motion is also known as the Law of __________.
The tendency of a moving object to keep moving in a straight line is known as __________.

short_questions.question: What is Newton's First Law of Motion?
Give an example of Newton's First Law in everyday life.
How does Newton's First Law apply to a hockey puck sliding on ice?
What role does friction play in Newton's First Law?
